subject: Computer Networking
title: IPsec: Securing the Network Layer
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on IPsec: Securing the Network Layer.

hook.text: TLS secures individual connections. IPsec secures all traffic between two points at the network layer.

buildup.text: IPsec encrypts and authenticates every IP packet. It works transparently — apps don't need to change.

discovery.text: It has two modes: transport encrypts the payload, tunnel wraps the entire packet inside a new encrypted one.

twist.text: Most VPNs use IPsec tunnel mode. Your device wraps all traffic and sends it through an encrypted tunnel.

climax.text: IPsec is complex to configure but powerful. It protects site-to-site links between offices worldwide.

punchline.text: IPsec encrypts everything at the IP level. Total network protection.

quiz.question: At which layer does IPsec operate?
